title: Raising the Mary Rose
content_html:
Raising the Mary Rose

How a sixteenth-century warship was recovered from the seabed

On 19 July 1545, English and French fleets were engaged in a sea battle off the coast of southern England in the area of water called the Solent, between Portsmouth and the Isle of Wight. Among the English vessels was a warship by the name of Mary Rose. Built in Portsmouth some 35 years earlier, she had had a long and successful fighting career, and was a favourite of King Henry VIII. Accounts of what happened to the ship vary: while witnesses agree that she was not hit by the French, some maintain that she was outdated, overladen and sailing too low in the water, others that she was mishandled by undisciplined crew. What is undisputed, however, is that the Mary Rose sank into the Solent that day, taking at least 500 men with her. After the battle, attempts were made to recover the ship, but these failed.

The Mary Rose came to rest on the seabed, lying on her starboard (right) side at an angle of approximately 60 degrees. The hull (the body of the ship) acted as a trap for the sand and mud carried by Solent currents. As a result, the starboard side filled rapidly, leaving the exposed port (left) side to be eroded by marine organisms and mechanical degradation. Because of the way the ship sank, nearly all of the starboard half survived intact. During the seventeenth and eighteenth centuries, the entire site became covered with a layer of hard grey clay, which minimised further erosion.

Then, on 16 June 1836, some fishermen in the Solent found that their equipment was caught on an underwater obstruction, which turned out to be the Mary Rose. Diver John Deane happened to be exploring another sunken ship nearby, and the fishermen approached him, asking him to free their gear. Deane dived down, and found the equipment caught on a timber protruding slightly from the seabed. Exploring further, he uncovered several other timbers and a bronze gun. Deane continued diving on the site intermittently until 1840, recovering several more guns, two bows, various timbers, part of a pump and various other small finds.

The Mary Rose then faded into obscurity for another hundred years. But in 1965, military historian and amateur diver Alexander McKee, in conjunction with the British Sub-Aqua Club, initiated a project called ‘Solent Ships’. While on paper this was a plan to examine a number of known wrecks in the Solent, what McKee really hoped for was to find the Mary Rose. Ordinary search techniques proved unsatisfactory, so McKee entered into collaboration with Harold E. Edgerton, professor of electrical engineering at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology. In 1967, Edgerton’s side-scan sonar systems revealed a large, unusually shaped object, which McKee believed was the Mary Rose.

Further excavations revealed stray pieces of timber and an iron gun. But the climax to the operation came when, on 5 May 1971, part of the ship’s frame was uncovered. McKee and his team now knew for certain that they had found the wreck, but were as yet unaware that it also housed a treasure trove of beautifully preserved artefacts. Interest ^ in the project grew, and in 1979, The Mary Rose Trust was formed, with Prince Charles as its President and Dr Margaret Rule its Archaeological Director. The decision whether or not to salvage the wreck was not an easy one, although an excavation in 1978 had shown that it might be possible to raise the hull. While the original aim was to raise the hull if at all feasible, the operation was not given the go-ahead until January 1982, when all the necessary information was available.

An important factor in trying to salvage the Mary Rose was that the remaining hull was an open shell. This led to an important decision being taken: namely to carry out the lifting operation in three very distinct stages. The hull was attached to a lifting frame via a network of bolts and lifting wires. The problem of the hull being sucked back downwards into the mud was overcome by using 12 hydraulic jacks. These raised it a few centimetres over a period of several days, as the lifting frame rose slowly up its four legs. It was only when the hull was hanging freely from the lifting frame, clear of the seabed and the suction effect of the surrounding mud, that the salvage operation progressed to the second stage. In this stage, the lifting frame was fixed to a hook attached to a crane, and the hull was lifted completely clear of the seabed and transferred underwater into the lifting cradle. This required precise positioning to locate the legs into the stabbing guides’ of the lifting cradle. The lifting cradle was designed to fit the hull using archaeological survey drawings, and was fitted with air bags to provide additional cushioning for the hull’s delicate timber framework. The third and final stage was to lift the entire structure into the air, by which time the hull was also supported from below. Finally, on 11 October 1982, millions of people around the world held their breath as the timber skeleton of the Mary Rose was lifted clear of the water, ready to be returned home to Portsmouth.

questions.question: There is some doubt about what caused the Mary Rose to sink.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–4

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 1–4 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “some doubt” (sự nghi ngờ/không chắc chắn), “what caused”, “Mary Rose to sink” (nguyên nhân chìm)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “Accounts of what happened to the ship vary: while witnesses agree that she was not hit by the French, some maintain that she was outdated, overladen and sailing too low in the water, others that she was mishandled by undisciplined crew”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “Accounts... vary” (các lời kể/báo cáo khác nhau) và việc người thì cho rằng tàu quá tải (overladen), người lại bảo do thủy thủ đoàn thiếu kỷ luật (mishandled by undisciplined crew) chứng tỏ nguyên nhân chính xác khiến tàu chìm vẫn còn những sự nghi ngờ, chưa đồng nhất.

=> Đáp án: TRUE

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question: The Mary Rose was the only ship to sink in the battle of 19 July 1545.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “the only ship to sink” (con tàu duy nhất bị chìm), “battle of 19 July 1545”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Toàn bộ bài đọc (hoặc các đoạn kể về trận chiến ngày 19/07/1545).

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ tập trung kể về sự kiện tàu Mary Rose bị chìm vào ngày này, hoàn toàn không cung cấp bất kỳ thông tin nào để xác định xem nó có phải là con tàu "duy nhất" bị chìm trong trận chiến đó hay không.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.question: Most of one side of the Mary Rose lay undamaged under the sea.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “King Henry VIII”, “captain”, “to blame” (đổ lỗi/khiển trách), “sinking”

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1 hoặc Đoạn 2 (Phần nói về phản ứng của nhà vua): Trong bài đọc gốc có đoạn: “Witnesses agree that she was not hit by the French... King Henry VIII himself watched the disaster unfold from Southsea Castle, but there is no record of his personal opinion regarding who was responsible.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ nói rằng Vua Henry VIII đã tự mình chứng kiến thảm họa từ lâu đài Southsea, nhưng không có tài liệu nào ghi lại ý kiến cá nhân của ông về việc ai là người phải chịu trách nhiệm (no record of his personal opinion regarding who was responsible). Do đó, việc ông có đổ lỗi cho vị thuyền trưởng hay không là không thể xác định.

=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.question: Alexander McKee knew that the wreck would contain many valuable historical objects.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “completely destroyed” (bị phá hủy hoàn toàn), “hit the seabed” (chạm đáy biển)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2 hoặc Đoạn 3 (Phần mô tả tình trạng xác tàu dưới biển): “The ship then sank into the mud, which preserved much of the hull... half of the Mary Rose lay survived, intact and filled with clay.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ ra rằng con tàu chìm xuống bùn và lớp bùn này đã bảo tồn phần lớn thân tàu (preserved much of the hull), một nửa con tàu vẫn sống sót và còn nguyên vẹn (lay survived, intact). Vì vậy, thông tin tàu bị "phá hủy hoàn toàn" (completely destroyed) là sai.

=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.question: A search for the Mary Rose was launched.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 5–8

Match each statement with the correct option, A–G.

Write the correct letter in boxes 5–8 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“A search”, “launched” (được khởi động/bắt đầu)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “But in 1965, military historian and amateur diver Alexander McKee... initiated a project called ‘Solent Ships’. While on paper this was a plan to examine a number of known wrecks... what McKee really hoped for was to find the Mary Rose”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “initiated a project” đồng nghĩa với “launched”. Việc Alexander McKee khởi động dự án này với mục đích thực sự là tìm kiếm tàu Mary Rose (find the Mary Rose) diễn ra vào năm 1965 (tương ứng với ký tự C trong danh sách đáp án của bạn).

=> Đáp án: C

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.
featureStatements: A search for the Mary Rose was launched.
One person’s exploration of the Mary Rose site stopped
It was agreed that the hull of the Mary Rose should be raised.
The site of the Mary Rose was found by chance.

questions.questionHtml:
List of features

A  1836

B  1840

C  1965

D  1967

E  1971

F  1979

G  1982

5_______ A search for the Mary Rose was launched.

6_______ One person’s exploration of the Mary Rose site stopped

7_______ It was agreed that the hull of the Mary Rose should be raised.

8_______ The site of the Mary Rose was found by chance.

questions.question: One person's exploration of the Mary Rose site stopped.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“one person’s exploration”, “stopped” (bị dừng lại)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “Deane continued diving on the site intermittently until 1840, recovering several more guns, two bows... and various other small finds.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Trích dẫn chỉ ra chuyên gia lặn Deane đã tiếp tục lặn ở khu vực này một cách ngắt quãng cho đến năm (until) 1840. Từ "cho đến năm 1840" đồng nghĩa với việc hành trình khám phá của ông đã dừng lại vào mốc thời gian này (tương ứng với ký tự B trong danh sách đáp án của bạn).

=> Đáp án: B

questions.question: It was agreed that the hull of the Mary Rose should be raised.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “agreed” (được đồng ý/chấp thuận), “hull”, “should be raised” (nên được nâng lên/trục vớt)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn cuối (phần quyết định trục vớt): “The operation was not given the go-ahead until January 1982, when all the information was available to write a success-forecast for the project.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “given the go-ahead” (được bật đèn xanh/cho phép thực hiện) đồng nghĩa với “agreed”. Sự kiện này chính thức được thông qua vào tháng 1 năm 1982 (tương ứng với ký tự G).

=> Đáp án: G

questions.question: The site of the Mary Rose was found by chance.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “site”, “found by chance” (tìm thấy tình cờ/ngẫu nhiên)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 3: “Then, on 16 June 1836, some fishermen in the Solent found that their equipment was caught on an underwater obstruction, which turned out to be the Mary Rose.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Việc một số người đánh cá bị vướng lưới/dụng cụ vào một chướng ngại vật dưới nước, sau đó mới hóa ra đó chính là tàu Mary Rose chứng tỏ vị trí xác tàu được phát hiện hoàn toàn ngẫu nhiên và tình cờ. Sự kiện này diễn ra vào ngày 16 tháng 6 năm 1836 (tương ứng với ký tự A).

=> Đáp án: A

questions.question: 9. _________attached to hull by wires
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 9–14

Complete the notes below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“attached to hull”, “by wires” (bằng các sợi dây)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “The hull was attached to a lifting frame via a network of bolts and lifting wires”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Đề bài tìm danh từ chỉ vật thể được gắn vào thân tàu (hull) thông qua hệ thống dây cáp nâng (lifting wires). Trong cấu trúc câu của bài đọc, vật thể đó chính là lifting frame (khung nâng).

=> Đáp án: a lifting frame

_blockConfig.noteContent:
• [9] attached to hull by wires
 • [10] to prevent hull being sucked into mud 
 • legs are placed into [11] 
 • hull is lowered into [12] 
 • [13] used as extra protection for the hull

questions.questionHtml:
• 9_______ attached to hull by wires
  • 10_______ to prevent hull being sucked into mud
  • legs are placed into 11_______
  • hull is lowered into 12_______
  • 13_______ used as extra protection for the hull

questions.text: 10. _________  to prevent hull being sucked into mud
questions.question: 10. _________  to prevent hull being sucked into mud
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “prevent”, “hull being sucked into mud” (ngăn thân tàu bị hút vào bùn)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “The problem of the hull being sucked back downwards into the mud was overcome by using 12 hydraulic jacks.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “prevent... being sucked into” đồng nghĩa với cấu trúc giải quyết vấn đề “problem of... being sucked back downwards into... was overcome by”. Công cụ được sử dụng để xử lý việc này là hydraulic jacks (kích thủy lực).

=> Đáp án: hydraulic jacks

questions.question: legs are placed into 11. _________
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Legs”, “placed into” (được đặt/định vị vào)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “This required precise positioning to locate the legs into the ‘stabbing guides’ of the lifting cradle”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “placed into” đồng nghĩa với cụm “locate... into”. Danh từ chỉ nơi các chân cấu trúc (legs) được đưa vào chính là cụm từ nằm trong dấu ngoặc kép của bài đọc: stabbing guides.

=> Đáp án: stabbing guides

questions.question: hull is lowered into  12. _________
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Hull”, “lowered into” (được hạ/chuyển vào)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...and the hull was lifted completely clear of the seabed and transferred underwater into the lifting cradle”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “lowered into” (hạ xuống/đặt vào vị trí thấp hơn bên dưới dòng nước) đồng nghĩa với hành động “transferred underwater into”. Nơi tiếp nhận thân tàu lúc này chính là chiếc lifting cradle (nôi nâng).

=> Đáp án: lifting cradle

questions.text: 13. _________ used as extra protection for the hull
questions.question: 13. _________ used as extra protection for the hull
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “used as”, “extra protection” (sự bảo vệ/giảm chấn bổ sung)

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6: “...and was fitted with air bags to provide additional cushioning for the hull’s delicate timber framework.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “extra protection” đồng nghĩa với “additional cushioning” (lớp đệm bổ sung bảo vệ khung gỗ nhạy cảm). Vật mút/đệm được trang bị để thực hiện chức năng này là các air bags (túi khí).

=> Đáp án: air bags
